info.title: U.S. National Debt Held by China
info.subtitle: Interactive Dataset, SQL Analysis, Singine Registration, and SilkPage Rendering

copyright.holder: Recorded under Singine Cortex Bridge — conversation asset

abstract.para: A multi-turn conversation session producing a structured dataset of U.S. national debt held by China (1990–2220), Hive DDL, twelve analytical SQL queries, eight SPARQL queries executed through Singine's cortex bridge, and an interactive Chart.js HTML timeline visualization with drill-down event context.

This document registers the session in the Singine Cortex Bridge SQLite database, records a Logseq page in the active graph (fa-KfK-HDYAT-KRNL-421b), and renders through SilkPage 0.6.0 using the DocBook 5.2 XSL stylesheet pipeline (xhtml5/docbook.xsl).

preface.title: Session Overview
preface.para: This document captures a five-turn conversation session. Each turn produced discrete, durable artefacts that are registered in the Singine Cortex Bridge (/tmp/china_debt_singine.db) and cross-referenced in the active Logseq graph.

row.entry: Provide a detailed structured JSON dataset of US national debt held by China with historical figures and 20-decade projections
113-entry JSON dataset with date, amount_held_by_china, event_description, projection_flag
Write the dataset to a parquet file
Load into Hive and generate useful SQL using singine's SPARQL engine
Hive DDL, 12 analytical SQL queries, singine bridge ingestion + 8 SPARQL queries
Create a visually appealing HTML file to visualise on a timeline with drill-down; keep event context in a separate parquet file with JSON equivalent
Register this conversation under singine and use silkpage to render the output as DocBook 5.2-compatible website
register_and_render.py → DocBook 5.2 XML + xsltproc render via silkpage xhtml5/docbook.xsl

section.title: Artefact Inventory
section.para: All artefacts reside in the working directory of the conversation session.

chapter.title: Dataset: U.S. Treasury Holdings by China, 1990–2220
chapter.para: The dataset comprises 113 observations stored as Apache Parquet (us_debt_held_by_china.parquet). 87 records are historical (sourced from U.S. Treasury TIC data); 26 are base-case projections with explicit assumption notes.

row.entry: USD billions, rounded to nearest billion
Event context, estimation note, or projection assumption

listitem.para: 1994-01-01 — $35B: China pegs RMB to USD at 8.28; structural accumulation begins.
2001-12-11 — $82B: China accedes to WTO; export boom drives rapid Treasury accumulation.
2008-09-15 — $585B: Lehman collapse; flight-to-quality surge.
2013-11-01 — $1,317B: All-time peak. China holds 7.9% of total US public debt.
2022-02-24 — $1,055B: Russia-Ukraine invasion; Western sanctions on Russian central bank reserves change China's strategic calculus.
2022-12-31 — $867B: Record annual decline of $201B.
2024-12-31 — $759B: Near 14-year low; 42% below peak.

row.entry: Estimate; TIC systematic reporting incomplete for early 1990s. China holds minimal U.S. Treasuries, …
Estimate; Gulf War spurs dollar strength. China's export economy expanding but Treasury holdings rem…
Estimate; Deng Xiaoping's Southern Tour accelerates market reforms and export growth, beginning the …
Estimate; China's current account surplus begins structural expansion. Foreign exchange reserves gro…
China unifies exchange rate and effectively pegs RMB to USD at approximately 8.28. This policy locks…

chapter.title: Hive DDL and Analytical SQL
chapter.para: Twelve analytical SQL queries were generated for Hive, Spark SQL, Presto/Trino, and DuckDB. The source files are china_debt_hive.ddl and china_debt_queries.sql.

section.title: Hive External Table DDL
SQL Query Catalogue

row.entry: Named crisis and event spot-check (8 dates)
Estimated share of total foreign holdings over time

chapter.title: Singine Bridge Registration
chapter.para: The conversation and all artefacts are registered in the Singine Cortex Bridge SQLite database at /tmp/china_debt_singine.db. The bridge uses a five-table RDF-approximating schema: sources, entities, statements, fragments, and query_templates.

section.title: SPARQL Queries (Executed via singine bridge sparql)
section.para: Singine's SPARQL-to-SQL translator supports five pattern shapes. All eight queries below match supported patterns and executed successfully against the bridge database.

note.para: To replay interactively: singine bridge sparql --db /tmp/china_debt_singine.db "SELECT ?s ?o WHERE { ?s <http://singine.io/ontology#amountHeldBillionsUSD> ?o . } LIMIT 10"

chapter.title: Interactive HTML Visualization
chapter.para: The file china_debt_timeline.html is a fully self-contained interactive visualization (no server required) generated by generate_timeline_viz.py.

listitem.para: Dark-theme Chart.js 4.4 line chart with gradient fill under the historical series (solid blue) and a dashed amber projection line.
Color-coded scatter event markers (six categories) overlaid on the chart — fully clickable.
Three view range controls: 1990–2030 / 1990–2060 / Full Timeline (to 2220).
Category filter buttons: Crisis · Policy · Trade · Geopolitical · Records · All.
Horizontal scrollable event timeline strip with significance-height bars.
Slide-out detail panel with full narrative, economic indicators grid, significance dots, source note, and prev/next navigation.
Six KPI cards: all-time peak ($1.317T), current ($759B), decline (−42%), record drop (−$201B), % of total debt (2.1%), trend (REDUCING).

section.title: Events Context Parquet
section.para: 22 key events are stored in china_debt_events_context.parquet (and china_debt_events_context.json) with the following enriched fields:

listitem.para: event_id, category, headline
description — 200–400 word narrative per event
significance — 1–5 rating
impact_direction — INCREASE / DECREASE / STABLE / PEAK
related_indicators_json — era-specific economic indicators
source_note, projection_flag

appendix.title: Rendering Pipeline
appendix.para: This document was rendered using the following pipeline:

listitem.para: register_and_render.py generates this china-us-debt.xml (DocBook 5.2).
xsltproc (libxslt XSLT_VERSION=10135) applies silkpage/tools/docbook-xsl/xhtml5/docbook.xsl.
Output is written to silkpage/www/silkpage.markupware.com/publish/china-debt-timeline/.
A Logseq page is written to the active graph (fa-KfK-HDYAT-KRNL-421b) at pages/China US Debt Timeline.md.
The conversation is registered in the Singine Cortex Bridge SQLite DB as ConversationAsset with singine:produces links to all artefacts.

appendix.programlisting: # Render command (executed by register_and_render.py) xsltproc \ --nonet \ --stringparam html.stylesheet ../css/china-debt.css \ --stringparam section.autolabel 1 \ --stringparam toc.section.depth 2 \ --stringparam chunker.output.encoding UTF-8 \ --output index.html \ silkpage/tools/docbook-xsl/xhtml5/docbook.xsl \ china-us-debt.xml
